title: What is React.memo?
description: What does React.memo do?
options: Memoizes a functional component to skip re-renders if props haven't changed
Creates a memo note in the DOM
correct_answer: Memoizes a functional component to skip re-renders if props haven't changed

title: What is the useId hook?
description: What does the useId hook provide in React 18?
options: Generates unique IDs for accessibility attributes that are stable across server and client
Creates a unique key for list items
correct_answer: Generates unique IDs for accessibility attributes that are stable across server and client

title: What is useSyncExternalStore?
description: What is the purpose of useSyncExternalStore?
options: Subscribes to an external store in a way compatible with concurrent rendering
correct_answer: Subscribes to an external store in a way compatible with concurrent rendering

title: What is useInsertionEffect?
description: When should useInsertionEffect be used?
options: For injecting styles before DOM mutations, primarily used by CSS-in-JS libraries
For inserting elements into the DOM
correct_answer: For injecting styles before DOM mutations, primarily used by CSS-in-JS libraries

title: Difference between useEffect and useLayoutEffect?
description: How do useEffect and useLayoutEffect differ?
options: useLayoutEffect fires synchronously after DOM mutations but before paint; useEffect fires after paint
correct_answer: useLayoutEffect fires synchronously after DOM mutations but before paint; useEffect fires after paint

title: What is React.lazy?
description: What does React.lazy enable?
options: Code splitting by lazily loading a component when it's first rendered
correct_answer: Code splitting by lazily loading a component when it's first rendered

title: What is the Suspense component?
description: What does Suspense do?
options: Shows a fallback UI while waiting for lazy-loaded components or async data to resolve
correct_answer: Shows a fallback UI while waiting for lazy-loaded components or async data to resolve

title: What is the useTransition hook?
description: What does useTransition do?
options: Marks a state update as non-urgent so the UI stays responsive during transitions
correct_answer: Marks a state update as non-urgent so the UI stays responsive during transitions

title: What is useDeferredValue?
description: What does useDeferredValue do?
options: Defers re-rendering a non-urgent part of the UI, showing stale content while new content loads
correct_answer: Defers re-rendering a non-urgent part of the UI, showing stale content while new content loads

title: What is the children prop?
description: What does props.children represent?
options: The content passed between the opening and closing tags of a component
correct_answer: The content passed between the opening and closing tags of a component

title: What is prop drilling?
description: What is the problem of prop drilling?
options: Passing props through many intermediate components that don't need them
correct_answer: Passing props through many intermediate components that don't need them

title: What is a React Fragment?
description: What does <></> or <Fragment> do?
options: Groups multiple elements without adding an extra DOM node
correct_answer: Groups multiple elements without adding an extra DOM node

title: What is the key prop used for?
description: Why is the key prop important in lists?
options: Helps React identify which items changed, were added, or removed for efficient reconciliation
correct_answer: Helps React identify which items changed, were added, or removed for efficient reconciliation

title: What is a controlled component?
description: What makes a form component controlled in React?
options: Its value is driven by React state and updated via onChange handler
correct_answer: Its value is driven by React state and updated via onChange handler

title: What is an uncontrolled component?
description: How does an uncontrolled component work?
options: The DOM itself manages the form data, accessed via refs
correct_answer: The DOM itself manages the form data, accessed via refs

title: What happens when setState is called?
description: What does React do when you call setState or a state setter?
options: Schedules a re-render with the new state value
correct_answer: Schedules a re-render with the new state value

title: What is the virtual DOM?
description: How does the virtual DOM work?
options: A lightweight in-memory representation of the real DOM that React diffs to minimize actual DOM updates
correct_answer: A lightweight in-memory representation of the real DOM that React diffs to minimize actual DOM updates

title: What is reconciliation?
description: What is React's reconciliation process?
options: The algorithm React uses to diff the old and new virtual DOM trees and update the real DOM efficiently
correct_answer: The algorithm React uses to diff the old and new virtual DOM trees and update the real DOM efficiently

title: What is a portal in React?
description: What does ReactDOM.createPortal do?
options: Renders children into a DOM node that exists outside the parent component's DOM hierarchy
correct_answer: Renders children into a DOM node that exists outside the parent component's DOM hierarchy

title: What is the useReducer hook?
description: When should you use useReducer over useState?
options: When state logic is complex with multiple sub-values or when next state depends on previous state
correct_answer: When state logic is complex with multiple sub-values or when next state depends on previous state

title: What is the useRef hook?
description: What are common use cases for useRef?
options: Accessing DOM elements directly and storing mutable values that don't trigger re-renders
correct_answer: Accessing DOM elements directly and storing mutable values that don't trigger re-renders

title: What is the useImperativeHandle hook?
description: What does useImperativeHandle do?
options: Customizes the instance value exposed to parent components when using forwardRef
correct_answer: Customizes the instance value exposed to parent components when using forwardRef

title: What is forwardRef?
description: What does React.forwardRef do?
options: Allows a parent component to pass a ref to a child component's DOM element
correct_answer: Allows a parent component to pass a ref to a child component's DOM element

title: What is the StrictMode component?
description: What does React.StrictMode do?
options: Activates additional checks and warnings for descendants in development mode
correct_answer: Activates additional checks and warnings for descendants in development mode

title: What is the difference between state and props?
description: How do state and props differ?
options: State is owned and managed by the component; props are passed from parent and are read-only
State is global; props are local
correct_answer: State is owned and managed by the component; props are passed from parent and are read-only

title: What is the useDebugValue hook?
description: What does useDebugValue do?
options: Displays a label for custom hooks in React DevTools
correct_answer: Displays a label for custom hooks in React DevTools

title: What is the componentDidMount equivalent in hooks?
description: How do you replicate componentDidMount with hooks?
options: useEffect with an empty dependency array: useEffect(() => {}, [])
correct_answer: useEffect with an empty dependency array: useEffect(() => {}, [])

title: What is the componentWillUnmount equivalent in hooks?
description: How do you handle cleanup with hooks?
options: Return a cleanup function from useEffect: useEffect(() => { return () => cleanup(); }, [])
correct_answer: Return a cleanup function from useEffect: useEffect(() => { return () => cleanup(); }, [])

title: What are React Server Components?
description: What do React Server Components enable?
options: Components that render on the server with zero client-side JavaScript bundle, can access backend resources directly
correct_answer: Components that render on the server with zero client-side JavaScript bundle, can access backend resources directly

title: What is React hydration?
description: What does hydration mean in React SSR?
options: Attaching event listeners and making server-rendered HTML interactive on the client
correct_answer: Attaching event listeners and making server-rendered HTML interactive on the client

title: What is the Error Boundary pattern?
description: How do Error Boundaries work?
options: Class components that catch JavaScript errors in their child tree and display a fallback UI
correct_answer: Class components that catch JavaScript errors in their child tree and display a fallback UI

title: What is the compound component pattern?
description: What is the compound component pattern?
options: Components that work together sharing implicit state, like <Select> and <Option>
correct_answer: Components that work together sharing implicit state, like <Select> and <Option>

title: What is React.cloneElement?
description: What does React.cloneElement do?
options: Clones a React element and merges new props into the clone
correct_answer: Clones a React element and merges new props into the clone

title: What is the render prop pattern?
description: What is a render prop?
options: A prop whose value is a function that returns a React element, used for sharing logic
correct_answer: A prop whose value is a function that returns a React element, used for sharing logic

title: What is Concurrent Mode in React?
description: What does Concurrent Mode enable?
options: Allows React to work on multiple state updates simultaneously, prioritizing urgent updates
correct_answer: Allows React to work on multiple state updates simultaneously, prioritizing urgent updates

title: What is automatic batching in React 18?
description: How does automatic batching work?
options: React 18 batches all state updates (including those in promises and timeouts) into a single re-render
correct_answer: React 18 batches all state updates (including those in promises and timeouts) into a single re-render

title: What is React.createElement?
description: What does React.createElement do?
options: Creates a React element (the underlying call that JSX compiles to)
correct_answer: Creates a React element (the underlying call that JSX compiles to)

title: What is the difference between React.Component and React.PureComponent?
description: How do they differ?
options: PureComponent implements shouldComponentUpdate with a shallow prop and state comparison
correct_answer: PureComponent implements shouldComponentUpdate with a shallow prop and state comparison

title: What is the flushSync function?
description: What does flushSync do in React?
options: Forces React to flush pending state updates synchronously, triggering immediate DOM updates
correct_answer: Forces React to flush pending state updates synchronously, triggering immediate DOM updates

title: What is React.startTransition?
description: What does startTransition do?
options: Marks a state update as a transition (non-urgent), allowing the UI to remain responsive
correct_answer: Marks a state update as a transition (non-urgent), allowing the UI to remain responsive

title: How does useCallback improve performance?
description: When should you use useCallback?
options: When passing callbacks to memoized child components, to prevent unnecessary re-renders by keeping the same function reference
correct_answer: When passing callbacks to memoized child components, to prevent unnecessary re-renders by keeping the same function reference

title: What is the dangerouslySetInnerHTML prop?
description: What does dangerouslySetInnerHTML do?
options: Sets raw HTML content inside an element (equivalent to innerHTML), with XSS risk
correct_answer: Sets raw HTML content inside an element (equivalent to innerHTML), with XSS risk

title: What is prop types in React?
description: What does the PropTypes library do?
options: Provides runtime type checking for component props during development
correct_answer: Provides runtime type checking for component props during development

title: What is the useContext hook?
description: What does useContext provide?
options: Reads the value of a context created by React.createContext, avoiding prop drilling
correct_answer: Reads the value of a context created by React.createContext, avoiding prop drilling

title: What are custom hooks?
description: What defines a custom hook?
options: A function starting with 'use' that can call other hooks to encapsulate reusable stateful logic
correct_answer: A function starting with 'use' that can call other hooks to encapsulate reusable stateful logic

title: What is React Router's <Outlet>?
description: What does <Outlet> render?
options: Renders the matching child route component in a nested route layout
correct_answer: Renders the matching child route component in a nested route layout

title: What is React Router's useNavigate?
description: What does useNavigate provide?
options: A function to programmatically navigate to different routes
correct_answer: A function to programmatically navigate to different routes

title: What is React Router's useParams?
description: What does useParams return?
options: An object of key-value pairs from the current URL's dynamic segments
correct_answer: An object of key-value pairs from the current URL's dynamic segments

title: What is the useSearchParams hook?
description: What does useSearchParams do in React Router?
options: Reads and modifies the URL query string parameters
correct_answer: Reads and modifies the URL query string parameters

title: What is the purpose of React DevTools?
description: What can you do with React DevTools?
options: Inspect the component tree, props, state, hooks, and profile performance of a React app
correct_answer: Inspect the component tree, props, state, hooks, and profile performance of a React app

title: What is the React Profiler?
description: What does the Profiler component measure?
options: Measures how often a React app renders and the cost of rendering, helping identify bottlenecks
correct_answer: Measures how often a React app renders and the cost of rendering, helping identify bottlenecks

title: Build a useLocalStorage custom hook.
description: Create a custom React hook that syncs state with localStorage, with initial value support and automatic JSON serialization.

title: Build a useDebounce custom hook.
description: Create a custom React hook that debounces a value by a given delay.

title: Build a useFetch custom hook.
description: Create a custom React hook that fetches data from a URL and returns data, loading, and error states.

title: Build a usePrevious custom hook.
description: Create a hook that returns the previous value of a state variable.

title: Implement infinite scroll with hooks.
description: Write a React component that loads more items when the user scrolls to the bottom of the page.

title: Build a useOnClickOutside hook.
description: Create a custom hook that detects clicks outside a referenced element.

title: What is React.createContext?
description: How do you create and use a context?
options: Creates a Context object with Provider and Consumer; components can subscribe via useContext
correct_answer: Creates a Context object with Provider and Consumer; components can subscribe via useContext

title: What is the shouldComponentUpdate lifecycle?
description: What does shouldComponentUpdate control?
options: Determines if a component should re-render by returning true or false based on next props/state
correct_answer: Determines if a component should re-render by returning true or false based on next props/state

title: What is getDerivedStateFromProps?
description: When is getDerivedStateFromProps called?
options: Called before every render to update state based on changes in props; returns new state or null
correct_answer: Called before every render to update state based on changes in props; returns new state or null

title: What is the useOptimistic hook?
description: What does the useOptimistic hook (React 19) do?
options: Shows an optimistic UI state while an async action is in progress, reverting on failure
correct_answer: Shows an optimistic UI state while an async action is in progress, reverting on failure

title: What is the 'use' hook in React 19?
description: What does the 'use' hook do?
options: Reads the value of a resource like a Promise or Context, can be called conditionally unlike other hooks
correct_answer: Reads the value of a resource like a Promise or Context, can be called conditionally unlike other hooks

title: What is the useFormStatus hook?
description: What does useFormStatus provide in React 19?
options: Returns the status of the parent form (pending, data, method, action) for showing loading states
correct_answer: Returns the status of the parent form (pending, data, method, action) for showing loading states

title: What is React's batching behavior?
description: When does React batch state updates?
options: React 18+ batches all state updates including those in promises, setTimeout, and native event handlers
correct_answer: React 18+ batches all state updates including those in promises, setTimeout, and native event handlers

title: Why should you not call hooks conditionally?
description: What is the rules of hooks regarding conditional calls?
options: Hooks must be called in the same order every render; conditional calls break this and cause bugs
correct_answer: Hooks must be called in the same order every render; conditional calls break this and cause bugs

title: What is React Fiber?
description: What is the Fiber architecture?
options: A complete rewrite of React's reconciliation algorithm enabling incremental rendering and prioritization
correct_answer: A complete rewrite of React's reconciliation algorithm enabling incremental rendering and prioritization

title: What is selective hydration?
description: What does selective hydration enable in React 18?
options: Hydrates parts of the page independently based on user interaction priority, without waiting for all JS to load
correct_answer: Hydrates parts of the page independently based on user interaction priority, without waiting for all JS to load

title: What is streaming SSR?
description: What does streaming SSR provide in React 18?
options: Sends HTML to the browser in chunks as it's generated, improving Time to First Byte
correct_answer: Sends HTML to the browser in chunks as it's generated, improving Time to First Byte

title: Build a useWindowSize hook.
description: Create a custom hook that returns the current window width and height and updates on resize.

title: Build a useInterval hook.
description: Create a custom hook that sets up a setInterval that properly cleans up and handles changing delays.

title: Implement a toggle component.
description: Build a reusable Toggle component using the compound component pattern with Toggle, Toggle.On, Toggle.Off, and Toggle.Button.
